_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the ideation skill: does a real `/charness:ideation` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? ideation has no planner that emits required reads; its only script, scaffold_ideation_artifact.py, emits the OUTPUT artifact skeleton (## World Model / Verified Facts / Assumptions / Structured Questions / Open Questions / Next Step), not the living-model discipline. The references are a lens MENU: eight of nine are genuinely conditional (on-demand with triggers — agent-human, decision-question, effectuation, sequence-discipline, spec-boundary, structured-questions, truth-and-edge, world-modeling), applied only when the concept fits. The single engage-always spine is concept-architecture.md: it owns the main loop, the document discipline, and the maintenance discipline (rewrite obsolete parts rather than letting contradictory versions accumulate) that governs every representative living-model run. This calibration shipped a skill fix in the same pass: SKILL.md Workflow step 1 previously INLINED concept-architecture.md's content without routing to it at the point of need (the doc was only linked in the bottom References list), so a representative run could establish the model from the inline bullets and never open the doc — an unsatisfiable RCF floor. Step 1 now routes 'per references/concept-architecture.md' (preview-and-defer, the same point-of-need routing hitl/hotl/quality use for their engage-always docs) and the redundant inline was trimmed, making the doc the single source and the floor real. The prompt is pinned to a representative under-shaped concept that drives the living-model loop (bare /charness:ideation stalls — ideation needs a concept target, lens 2); it is kept general so it forces the core concept-architecture.md spine rather than over-triggering a specific conditional lens. declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); shared refs (generative-sequence.md, success-criteria-review.md) and the cross-skill taxonomy-axis-checkpoint.md are not skill-local so they are not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:ideation 아직 모양이 안 잡힌 새 워크플로 컨셉을 같이 다듬고 싶어 — 살아있는 concept model을 유지하면서 verified facts·assumptions·open questions·candidate direction을 분리하고, 대화가 진행되면 모델을 갱신해줘. 충분히 날카로워지면 ideation에 더 머물지 아니면 spec으로 넘길지까지 판단해줘.

agent-human-lens.md.rationale: SKILL.md lists 'agent-human lens' as one optional lens in Workflow step 2, selected when 'agents, APIs, CLI, and interface choices' are first-class design constraints for that particular concept.
agent-human-lens.md.trigger: when the concept includes agent-native surfaces, CLI/API/skill design decisions, or interface quality concerns

concept-architecture.md.rationale: The single engage-always spine. Workflow step 1 now establishes the living concept model 'per references/concept-architecture.md', which owns the main loop and document discipline; the scaffold script emits only the artifact skeleton, so the discipline for maintaining the model (rewrite obsolete parts, do not let contradictory versions accumulate, keep a stable canonical home) lives only in this doc. A representative concept-shaping run that never opens it skipped its own routing. The same-pass skill fix trimmed the redundant inline and added the point-of-need route so this floor is real, not aspirational.

decision-question-response.md.rationale: SKILL.md routes this conditionally: 'When the user asks a design or workflow decision-shaped question... answer with a decision frame'; this is a pattern match on the user's question shape, not a universal part of the workflow.
decision-question-response.md.trigger: when the user asks a decision-shaped question such as 'what do we need to decide?', 'which direction is better?', or 'what decision issues are open?'

effectuation.md.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly gates this: 'when uncertainty is high and the next move should start from available means, affordable loss, or early stakeholder commitment, borrow Saras Sarasvathy-style effectuation'.
effectuation.md.trigger: when the user's concept is highly uncertain and the next move should start from available means, affordable loss, or early stakeholder commitment

sequence-discipline.md.rationale: SKILL.md applies this conditionally for Workflow step 3 to resolve upstream questions; the reference guides decision ordering but is not invoked for every ideation.
sequence-discipline.md.trigger: when the concept has multiple interdependent decisions or when resolving the upstream question would materially change the rest of the design

spec-boundary.md.rationale: SKILL.md Workflow step 6 asks 'whether the work should stay in ideation, move to spec, or run a quick validation loop first'; this reference provides the handoff decision criteria, engaged only when the run reaches a ready-to-route boundary.
spec-boundary.md.trigger: at the end of ideation when deciding whether the concept is ready to move to spec, stay in ideation, or run validation first

structured-questions.md.rationale: SKILL.md presents this as opt-in: 'When the open questions are ones a downstream skill must act on, emit an opt-in Structured Questions section'; prose-only output stays valid, so the machine-legible schema this doc owns is engaged only on that downstream-handoff trigger.
structured-questions.md.trigger: when the ideation concludes with open questions that downstream skills (spec or impl) must act on, requiring machine-legible handoff format

truth-and-edge.md.rationale: SKILL.md presents truth and edge as optional shaping lenses chosen when the question fits; the skill asks to 'test demand/status quo/wedge/moat early' but only as one of many lenses depending on the concept's shape.
truth-and-edge.md.trigger: when the concept involves demand validation, competitive positioning, or requires assessment of customer pain and wedge/moat hypotheses

world-modeling.md.rationale: SKILL.md uses this conditionally: 'when the concept is really a system of actors, feedback loops, or leverage points'; the reference itself says 'do not force the split for a simple idea'.
world-modeling.md.trigger: when the concept involves systems, workflows with multiple stages, or when entity/stage separation would clarify contradictions
